$schema_note: Contronym / multi-sense resolution by sentence context. One root, several senses; the governing partner, object, or construct picks ONE. Where the context is decisive emit resolved-with-context; where it is not, emit pending (reason multi_sense or contronym or context_sensitive_needs_nahw). Same-root polyseme quarantines block a lexically-near but contextually-wrong sense from leaking onto a token. PENDING beats a wrong gloss.

rules.context_signal: paired antonym partner يَبْسُط (extends/expands) in a rizq (provision) context

decision_table.when: contrasted with يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ (extend vs withhold)
decision_table.reason: antonymic pairing with يَبْسُط forces the 'restrict' polarity, not 'is able' / 'ordains'

decision_table.when: no antonym partner / general ability context
decision_table.reason: default ability sense when no contrastive partner is present
ق-د-ر spans able / ordain / restrict; needs the governing context to choose

rules.context_signal: the object/construction fixes the verb sense

decision_table.when: with a blameworthy act as object

polyseme_quarantines.$note: Each pair: a token whose context-correct sense must be chosen, and a lexically-near same-root sibling whose sense must NOT leak onto it. These are resolved (context decisive) unless flagged pending.

entries.context: the religious sense is the lexical entry; 'peace' is a sibling, not this gloss

entries.correct: near / with / in the sight of
entries.context: ẓarf of place, not the obstinacy root-sense

negation_sense_rule.context_signal: لا followed by an indefinite NOUN in naṣb negates the genus, not a verb
negation_sense_rule.example_context: أَن لَّا مَلْجَأَ — لا negating the noun مَلْجَأ
negation_sense_rule.reason: لا النافية للجنس ('there is no X at all') vs verbal negation depends on whether a noun or verb follows; resolve by the following token's POS/case before glossing

general_rule: Where a contronym's partner or a polyseme's construct is present and decisive, resolve with context; otherwise emit pending(multi_sense | contronym | context_sensitive_needs_nahw). Never let a same-root sibling sense leak onto a token whose context names a different sense.
